title: Remembered depth preferences
story: As a developer, I want the roundtable to remember my topic depth preferences across sessions so that I don't have to re-establish my style every time.
acceptance_criteria: User preferences stored in ~/.isdlc/user-memory/profile.json
Preferences persist across projects
At topic transitions, roundtable acknowledges known preference and asks for confirmation

title: Project-specific topic history
story: As a team member, I want the project to remember how topics were handled in past analyses so that new sessions benefit from shared context.
acceptance_criteria: Project memory stored in .isdlc/roundtable-memory.json
Per-topic records include depth used and assumption counts
Shared across team members via version control

title: Conflict resolution between memory layers
story: As a developer, I want to be informed when my personal preferences conflict with project history so that I can make an informed choice about depth for this session.
acceptance_criteria: Both signals surfaced when user and project memory disagree
User chooses which to follow
Choice recorded in session record

title: Session record persistence
story: As a developer, I want each roundtable session's outcomes recorded automatically so that future sessions can learn from past behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Session record written to user and project memory after ROUNDTABLE_COMPLETE
Record captures per-topic depth, acknowledgment, and override status
Write failures do not block the roundtable completion signal

title: Manual memory compaction
story: As a developer, I want to compact my raw session logs into a summary when I choose so that memory reads stay fast without automatic background processing.
acceptance_criteria: isdlc memory compact command available
Supports --user and --project scopes
Aggregates raw sessions into compacted summary
Reports results to CLI

title: Graceful degradation without memory
story: As a developer, I want the roundtable to work normally when memory files are missing or corrupted so that memory is never a blocker.
acceptance_criteria: Missing files silently skipped
Corrupted files treated as missing
Partial data accepted where valid
No error messages shown to user

title: Performance warning for large memory
story: As a developer, I want to be warned when memory reads are getting slow so that I know to run compaction.
acceptance_criteria: Warning shown when memory reads exceed 1 second
Suggestion to run isdlc memory compact
Warning is conversational, not an error

title: Preference weight adjustment
story: As a developer, I want my preferences to strengthen when confirmed and weaken when overridden so that the memory adapts to my changing behavior over time.
acceptance_criteria: Override increments override_count and reduces weight
Confirmation increases weight
Stale preferences decay toward neutral
